role_title: Channel Partner Manager | Venture Capital & Growth Equity

location.office_time_required: unclear; relocation support to NYC or SF mentioned, in-person engagements emphasized

level_signals: Expected to quarterback commercial aspects of Ramp's most strategic relationships
Own commercial relationships with dozens of leading VC/growth equity firms

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Minimum 4 years of work experience with a strong foundation in Investing (Private Equity / Venture Capital), Management Consulting, Investment Banking, or Investor Relations
must_haves.qualifier: strong foundation in PE/VC, Management Consulting, IB, or Investor Relations

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Strong discovery skills, with a knack for identifying pain points and consultative selling
History as a top performer, regularly going above and beyond to support commercial outcomes of clients or investments
Dedication to tracking and improving performance and efficiency daily
Strong written and verbal communication skills, with excellent listening skills
Bias for action and a strong desire to work in a fast-paced startup environment
A strong cross-functional collaborator who can build relationships across the company

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Background in Business Development, Partnerships, or Partner Sales, particularly in roles involving investment firms or financial institutions
Background working at or covering software & payments companies

scope.surface: Financial Institutions Channel — VC & Growth Equity partner program for Ramp's corporate card and expense management platform
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS + corporate card / fintech; channel-led distribution through VC/investor referrals
primary_metrics_inferred: Net-new pipeline sourced through VC/growth equity partners
Number of active partner firms and stakeholder relationships
core_responsibilities: Own the commercial relationships with dozens of leading venture & growth equity firms
Drive revenue for Ramp by sourcing net-new prospective sales opportunities
Identify, build, and nurture relationships with multiple stakeholders within investment funds
Work cross-functionally across Ramp Leadership in Finance, Investor Relations, Sales, Growth, and other functions
Become an expert in Ramp's product, features, and workflows to communicate directly with C-level finance leaders

green_flags: Clear ownership scope: dozens of VC/growth equity firm relationships
Ramp has meaningful scale ($100B+ annualized spend, 50,000+ companies) providing credible partner value proposition
In-person engagements and relocation support signal investment in the role
Relocation support to NYC or SF offered
Rich benefits package including 100% medical/dental/vision, 401(k) match, generous parental leave
red_flags: Visa sponsorship not addressed in the JD
Location type is ambiguous — in-person emphasis and relocation support to NYC/SF implied but no explicit hybrid/onsite policy stated
Role archetype sits at the intersection of sales, BD, and partnerships — scope may evolve and success metrics are not clearly defined

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 4+ years requirement and strategic ownership scope)
location.type (inferred as hybrid from relocation support mention and in-person engagement emphasis; not explicitly stated)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and channel motion)

ambiguities: Location type is not explicitly stated as onsite or hybrid; 'relocation support to NYC or SF' and 'emphasis on in-person engagements' suggest physical presence is expected, but a formal policy is not described. Treated as hybrid.
Visa sponsorship is neither confirmed nor denied anywhere in the JD. Set to 'unclear'.
Role spans channel sales, BD, and strategic partnerships — archetype could be 'Growth (non-PM)' or 'BizOps / Strategy & Ops'; BizOps chosen as closest match given strategic/commercial framing.
